hypothesis: Nao's first complete episode: one brief viewer-acknowledgment beat inside an otherwise unreachable register, ending on a loop-friendly final frame, tests whether the 'almost, not quite' + seamless-loop combination drives repeat viewing per BRAND_IDENTITY.md's core concept.

render.notes: keyframe=https://huggingface.co/OnomaAIResearch/Illustrious-XL-v2.0/resolve/main/Illustrious-XL-v2.0.safetensors via fal-ai/lora, video=kling v2.5-turbo/pro duration=10s. duration: spec requested 12s, rendered 10s (Kling caps at 10s) on_screen_text: not composited into the video (requires a separate compositing pass) prompting: this checkpoint needs Danbooru-tag-style prompts (comma-separated tags, not prose) to render cleanly -- write image_prompt accordingly
spec_deviations: duration: spec requested 12s, rendered 10s (Kling caps at 10s)
prompting: this checkpoint needs Danbooru-tag-style prompts (comma-separated tags, not prose) to render cleanly -- write image_prompt accordingly

post_processing.step: on_screen_text compositing (manual, not part of render_content.py's automated output)
post_processing.method: Text rendered via Chrome headless screenshot of an HTML page (Didot serif, white text on a solid black background -- Chrome's --screenshot CLI flag does not produce true alpha transparency), then composited onto video.mp4 with ffmpeg: [text]format=rgba,colorkey=0x000000:0.20:0.10 (keys out the black background) -> fade in 7.0-7.6s, fade out 9.0-9.6s -> overlay onto the base video. Do not use ffmpeg's blend=screen mode for this: it introduced a magenta/purple color-range cast across the whole frame (a PC-range-vs-video-range mismatch between the PNG and the h264 video), confirmed by a before/after frame comparison -- colorkey+overlay avoided this entirely.
post_processing.verified: Extracted frames at 5.0s (no text, pre-fade-in) and 8.0s (text visible, correct color) via ffmpeg -ss + -frames:v 1, visually confirmed.
